validated_commands.test: uv run py.test tests/ --override-ini='addopts=' -p no:codspeed -k 'not benchmark'
validated_commands.benchmark: uv run py.test tests/test_processing.py tests/test_filters.py --codspeed -k benchmark --override-ini='addopts='
validated_commands.note: uv not available in this CI environment; use: pip install -e . --break-system-packages && pip install pytest pytest-xdist --break-system-packages

open_prs.title: [Perf Improver] perf: use np.dot for channel-weighted sums in process_block

closed_prs.outcome: MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-04-28. CodSpeed: +7% on momentary/short_term/lra individual benchmarks, +2% overall (below 10% threshold).
CLOSED without merge. CodSpeed +3% individual, 1% overall. Lesson: Python dispatch reductions don't exceed 10% threshold for this scale.
MERGED. Algorithmic O(n^2 log n) -> O(n log n) change showed 37% improvement in CodSpeed.

optimization_backlog.desc: Submitted PR: np.dot instead of np.sum(a*b) for channel-weighted sums in process_block and _process_quarter_block
optimization_backlog.impact: integrated -7%, momentary -16%, short_term -9%, lra -12% local; expect 5-12% CodSpeed individual benchmarks
optimization_backlog.notes: np.dot avoids temporary array allocation + direct BLAS dispatch for small channel vectors (1-5 elements)

optimization_backlog.desc: reset() zeros all MAX_BLOCKS (144000) elements even for short measurements; only need to zero [:block_index]
optimization_backlog.impact: 63x faster reset, ~143us saved per reset call (after 50 blocks)
optimization_backlog.notes: Doesn't help benchmarks (teardown not timed). Good user-facing improvement for repeated reset() calls. NOT CodSpeed-detectable.

optimization_backlog.desc: lk_log10() uses np.log10 for scalars; math.log10 is ~1.4x faster for scalar path. ~1% benchmark improvement only.
optimization_backlog.impact: ~1% per benchmark mode (too small for CodSpeed, probably)
optimization_backlog.notes: Return type must stay np.float64 to preserve type annotations. Smaller win than np.dot was.

optimization_backlog.desc: _calc_gating does O(block_index) numpy work per block when rel_threshold changes; O(n^2) total.

perf_notes: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = overall improvement below 10% threshold. Individual benchmarks may still show meaningful gains.
Only algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) or large constant-factor changes (e.g., np.dot 4-5x faster) reliably exceed 10% CodSpeed threshold.
np.sum(a*b) vs np.dot(a,b): for 2-element arrays, np.dot is 4.2x faster (avoids temp array + direct BLAS dispatch)
np.square(full_block) reuse (PR #95): +7% in momentary/short_term/lra per CodSpeed individual benchmarks (MERGED)
math.log10 is 1.4x faster than np.log10 for full lk_log10 function (scalar path), only ~1% benchmark improvement
pytest-codspeed teardown (reset()) is NOT included in benchmark timing
Benchmark: 48kHz, 2 channels, gate_size=19200 (0.4s), ~46 blocks per round
CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions - Python dispatch overhead matters significantly
uv not available in CI runner; use pip install --break-system-packages as fallback
checked_off_items: perf-assist/incremental-lra PR (checked off in monthly activity 2026-04)
PR #95 single-sq-pass review (checked off by maintainer in monthly activity 2026-04)

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: CodSpeed 'does not alter performance' = below 10% overall threshold. Individual benchmarks accessible via CodSpeed link at bottom of PR comment.
infrastructure_notes.codspeed_gap: CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions. Small constant-factor improvements need to be >7% to be reliably visible. np.dot (4-5x faster for small arrays) should show well.
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: All 4 modes: integrated, momentary, short_term, lra. 48kHz, 2ch, gate_size=19200, ~46 blocks/round.
infrastructure_notes.what_shows_in_codspeed: Algorithmic changes (O(n^2) -> O(n)) or dispatch elimination for frequently-called operations (np.dot vs np.sum). Sub-1% local improvements likely invisible in CodSpeed.
